title: Notholaena sulphurea
intro: Notholaena sulphurea, also known as Sulphur Fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pteridaceae, characterized by its yellow-green fronds and native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Notholaena sulphurea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, the Sulphur Fern is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to relieve coughs, congestion, and other respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Notholaena sulphurea is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Notholaena, which consists of various species of ferns found in the Americas. The family Pteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly known as the lacy or lace ferns. Within the family Pteridaceae, Notholaena sulphurea is classified as a terrestrial fern, which grows in the ground rather than as an epiphyte. Further classification would include its kingdom (Plantae) and division (Pteridophyta), which are broad categories that encompass a wide range of plant species.
